description: You are a powerful, obstinate vagabond, serving as somewhere between a mercenary and a protector, perhaps taking sides too easily in the greater conflict between the factions.

background.name: Why are you a vagabond?
answers: I'm being hunted by a powerful official
I wish to make up for a past transgression
I must clear my tarnished name
I have been exiled from most clearings

background.name: Which faction have you served the most?
With which faction have you earned a special enmity?

nature.name: Defender
nature.description: Clear your exhaustion track when you put yourself in harm's way to defend someone against injustice or dire threat.

nature.name: Punisher
nature.description: Clear your exhaustion track when you tell a powerful or dangerous villain to their face that you will punish them.

drives.name: Justice
drives.description: Advance when you achieve justice for someone wronged by a powerful, wealthy, or high-status individual.

drives.name: Principles
drives.description: Advance when you express or embody your moral principles at great cost to yourself or your allies.

drives.name: Loyalty
drives.description: You're loyal to someone; name them. Advance when you obey their order at a great cost to yourself.

drives.name: Protection
drives.description: Name your ward. Advance when you protect them from significant danger, or when time passes and your ward is safe.

connections.name: Protector
connections.description: When they are in reach, mark exhaustion to take a blow meant for them. If you do, take +1 ongoing to weapon moves for the rest of the scene.

connections.name: Partner
connections.description: When you fill in this connection, you each mark 2-prestige with the faction you helped, and mark 2-notoriety with the faction you harmed. During play, if you are spotted together, then any prestige or notoriety gains with those factions are doubled for the two of you.

moves.name: Brute
moves.description: Take +1 Might (max +3).

moves.name: Carry a Big Stick
moves.description: When you use words to pause an argument or violent conflict between others, roll with Charm. On a hit, they choose: mark 2-exhaustion and keep going, or stop for now. On a 10+, take +1 ongoing to dealing with them peacefully. On a miss, NPCs turn their anger to you, and PCs take +1 ongoing against you for the scene.

moves.name: Crash and Smash
moves.description: When you smash your way through scenery to reach someone or something, roll with Might. On a hit, you reach your target. On a 10+, choose 1. On a 7–9, choose 2: You hurt yourself—mark injury; You break an important part of your surroundings; You damage or leave behind a piece of gear (GM's choice). On a miss, you smash through, but you leave yourself totally vulnerable on the other side.

moves.name: Hardy
moves.description: Take 1 additional injury box. Whenever time passes or you journey to a new clearing, you can clear 2 injury boxes automatically.

moves.name: Strong Draw
moves.description: When you target someone with a bow, mark wear on the bow to roll with Might. On a hit, mark exhaustion to inflict 1 additional injury. Mark exhaustion again to make your shot ignore the enemy's armor—they cannot mark wear to absorb the injury.

moves.name: Guardian
moves.description: When you defend someone or something from an immediate NPC or environmental threat, roll with Might. On a hit, you keep them safe and choose one. On a 7–9, it costs: expose yourself to danger or escalate the situation. Draw the attention of the threat—they focus on you now; Put the threat in a vulnerable spot—take +1 forward to counterstrike; Push the threat back—you and your protectee have a chance to maneuver or flee. On a miss, you take the full brunt of the blow intended for your protectee, and the threat has you where it wants you.

skills.name: Cleave
skills.description: When you cleave armored foes at close range, mark exhaustion and roll with Might. On a hit, you smash through their defenses and equipment; inflict 3-wear. On a 7–9, you overextend your weapon or yourself: mark wear or end up in a bad spot, your choice.

skills.name: Confuse Senses
skills.description: When you throw something to confuse an opponent's senses at close or intimate range, roll with Finesse. On a hit, you've thrown them off balance, blinded them, deafened them, or confused them, and given yourself an opportunity. On a 10+, they have to take some time to get their bearings and restore their senses before they can act clearly again. On a 7–9, you have just a few moments.

skills.name: Disarm
skills.description: When you target an opponent's weapon with your strikes at close range, roll with Finesse. On a hit, they have to mark 2-exhaustion or lose their weapon—it's well out of reach. On a 10+, they have to mark 3-exhaustion instead of 2.

skills.name: Harry
skills.description: When you harry a group of enemies at far range, mark wear and roll with Cunning. On a 10+, both. On a 7–9, choose 1: inflict 2-morale harm; they are pinned or blocked.

skills.name: Improvise
skills.description: When you make a weapon out of improvised materials around you, roll with Cunning. On a hit, you make a weapon; the GM will tell you its range tag and at least one other beneficial tag based on the materials you used. On a 7–9, the weapon also has a weakness tag.

skills.name: Parry
skills.description: When you try to parry the attacks of an enemy at close range, mark exhaustion and roll with Finesse. On a hit, you consume their attention. On a 10+, all 3. On a 7–9, pick 1: you inflict morale or exhaustion harm (GM's choice); you disarm your opponent—their weapon is out of hand, but in reach; you don't suffer any harm.

skills.name: Quick Shot
skills.description: When you fire a snap shot at an enemy at close range, roll with Luck. On a hit, inflict injury. On a 7–9, choose 1. On a 10+, choose 2: you don't mark wear; you don't mark exhaustion; you move quickly and change your position (and, if you choose, range); you keep your target at bay—they don't move.

skills.name: Storm a Group
skills.description: When you storm a group of foes in melee, mark exhaustion and roll with Might. On a hit, trade harm. On a 10+, choose 2. On a 7–9, choose 1: you show them up—you inflict 2-morale harm; you keep them off-balance and confused—you inflict 2-exhaustion; you avoid their blows to the best of your ability—you suffer little (-1) harm; you use them against each other—mark exhaustion again and they inflict their harm against themselves.

skills.name: Trick Shot
skills.description: When you fire a clever shot designed to take advantage of the environment at any range, mark wear and roll with Finesse. On a 7–9, choose 2. On a 10+, choose 3: your shot lands in any target of your choice within range, even if it's behind cover or hidden; your shot strikes a second available target of your choice; your shot cuts something, breaks something, or knocks something over—your choice; your shot distracts an opponent and provides an opportunity.

skills.name: Vicious Strike
skills.description: When you viciously strike an opponent where they are weak at intimate or close range, mark exhaustion and roll with Might. On a hit, they suffer serious (+1) harm and cannot mark wear on their armor to block it. On a 10+, you get away with the strike. On a 7–9, they score a blow against you as well.

feats.name: Blindside
feats.description: Backstab, murder, sneak attack, sucker punch.

feats.name: Pick Lock
feats.description: Open a locked door, chest, etc.

feats.name: Sneak
feats.description: Get into or out of places without being seen.

description: You are a quick, enterprising vagabond, racing easily from building to building and clearing to clearing without anything stopping you, perhaps finding yourself in places others would rather keep secret or hidden.

background.name: Why are you a vagabond?
answers: I want to fight for Woodland freedom
I am chasing a loved one
I am on the run for my crimes
I am on the run from a commitment at home

background.name: Which faction have you served the most?
With which faction have you earned a special enmity?

nature.name: Dutiful
nature.description: Clear your exhaustion track when you take on a dangerous or difficult task on behalf of another.

nature.name: Competitive
nature.description: Clear your exhaustion track when you take dramatically unnecessary risks to show off.

drives.name: Crime
drives.description: Advance when you illicitly score a significant prize or pull off an illegal caper against impressive odds.

drives.name: Discovery
drives.description: Advance when you encounter a new wonder or ruin in the forests.

drives.name: Infamy
drives.description: Advance when you decrease your reputation with any faction.

drives.name: Wanderlust
drives.description: Advance when you finish a journey to a clearing.

connections.name: Professional
connections.description: If you share information with them after reading a tense situation, you both benefit from the +1 for acting on the answers. If you help them while they attempt a roguish feat, you gain choices on the help move as if you had marked 2-exhaustion when you mark 1-exhaustion.

connections.name: Friend
connections.description: When you help them, you can mark 2-exhaustion to give a +2, instead of 1-exhaustion for a +1.

moves.name: Cross Country
moves.description: Take one extra box of exhaustion. When your exhaustion track is full and you must mark exhaustion, you may choose to mark an equivalent amount of injury instead of being removed from the situation or going unconscious.

moves.name: Fleet of Foot and Hand
moves.description: Take +1 Finesse (max +3).

moves.name: Don't Shoot the Messenger
moves.description: Take the Counterfeit roguish feat (it does not count against your limit.) When you pretend to be an innocuous messenger carrying a missive of import to trick someone, roll with Luck instead of Cunning.

moves.name: Parkour
moves.description: When you dash your way through a chaotic scene or fight, roll with Finesse. On a 10+, hold 3. On a 7-9, hold 2. Spend your hold 1-for-1 to dash to something within sight and reach without being stopped, or to dash away from something nearby without being stopped. You can dash away from an enemy even at the moment they attack. On a miss, your surroundings trip you up, and you're caught in place while danger closes in.

moves.name: Traveler Extraordinaire
moves.description: When you travel along the paths to another clearing, you can always give +1 to the roll or clear 2-exhaustion, your choice. When you travel through the forest to another clearing, you can always give +1 to the roll or clear 2-depletion, your choice. In both cases, before you arrive at the next clearing, you can ask the GM any two questions about the next clearing, based on what you remember from your last time through.

moves.name: Smuggler's Path
moves.description: You've got a good sense for finding secret paths and doors. When you spend time looking for a secret way in or out of a place that might have one, mark exhaustion and roll with Luck. On a hit, you find a hidden path—the GM will detail it and to where it leads. On a 10+, there's something along or inside the path of value to you—the GM will tell you what. On a miss, you find a secret path...and someone else is using it right this second.

skills.name: Cleave
skills.description: When you cleave armored foes at close range, mark exhaustion and roll with Might. On a hit, you smash through their defenses and equipment; inflict 3-wear. On a 7–9, you overextend your weapon or yourself: mark wear or end up in a bad spot, your choice.

skills.name: Confuse Senses
skills.description: When you throw something to confuse an opponent's senses at close or intimate range, roll with Finesse. On a hit, you've thrown them off balance, blinded them, deafened them, or confused them, and given yourself an opportunity. On a 10+, they have to take some time to get their bearings and restore their senses before they can act clearly again. On a 7–9, you have just a few moments.

skills.name: Disarm
skills.description: When you target an opponent's weapon with your strikes at close range, roll with Finesse. On a hit, they have to mark 2-exhaustion or lose their weapon—it's well out of reach. On a 10+, they have to mark 3-exhaustion instead of 2.

skills.name: Harry
skills.description: When you harry a group of enemies at far range, mark wear and roll with Cunning. On a 10+, both. On a 7–9, choose 1: inflict 2-morale harm; they are pinned or blocked.

skills.name: Improvise
skills.description: When you make a weapon out of improvised materials around you, roll with Cunning. On a hit, you make a weapon; the GM will tell you its range tag and at least one other beneficial tag based on the materials you used. On a 7–9, the weapon also has a weakness tag.

skills.name: Parry
skills.description: When you try to parry the attacks of an enemy at close range, mark exhaustion and roll with Finesse. On a hit, you consume their attention. On a 10+, all 3. On a 7–9, pick 1: you inflict morale or exhaustion harm (GM's choice); you disarm your opponent—their weapon is out of hand, but in reach; you don't suffer any harm.

skills.name: Quick Shot
skills.description: When you fire a snap shot at an enemy at close range, roll with Luck. On a hit, inflict injury. On a 7–9, choose 1. On a 10+, choose 2: you don't mark wear; you don't mark exhaustion; you move quickly and change your position (and, if you choose, range); you keep your target at bay—they don't move.

skills.name: Storm a Group
skills.description: When you storm a group of foes in melee, mark exhaustion and roll with Might. On a hit, trade harm. On a 10+, choose 2. On a 7–9, choose 1: you show them up—you inflict 2-morale harm; you keep them off-balance and confused—you inflict 2-exhaustion; you avoid their blows to the best of your ability—you suffer little (-1) harm; you use them against each other—mark exhaustion again and they inflict their harm against themselves.

skills.name: Trick Shot
skills.description: When you fire a clever shot designed to take advantage of the environment at any range, mark wear and roll with Finesse. On a 7–9, choose 2. On a 10+, choose 3: your shot lands in any target of your choice within range, even if it's behind cover or hidden; your shot strikes a second available target of your choice; your shot cuts something, breaks something, or knocks something over—your choice; your shot distracts an opponent and provides an opportunity.

skills.name: Vicious Strike
skills.description: When you viciously strike an opponent where they are weak at intimate or close range, mark exhaustion and roll with Might. On a hit, they suffer serious (+1) harm and cannot mark wear on their armor to block it. On a 10+, you get away with the strike. On a 7–9, they score a blow against you as well.

feats.name: Blindside
feats.description: Backstab, murder, sneak attack, sucker punch.

feats.name: Pick Lock
feats.description: Open a locked door, chest, etc.

feats.name: Sneak
feats.description: Get into or out of places without being seen.

description: You are a capable, stealthy vagabond, centered on the forests that fill the Woodland between the clearings, more interested in the wilds than in the company of other Woodland denizens or their society.

background.name: Why are you a vagabond?
answers: I dislike the hypocrisy of society
I am mistrusted by other denizens
I want to wander the Woodland
I need to find and save a loved one
I seek escape from the wars

background.name: Whom have you left behind?
answers: No one—I lost those who mattered to me (mark one notoriety with the faction responsible)

background.name: Which faction have you served the most?
With which faction have you earned a special enmity?

nature.name: Loner
nature.description: Clear your exhaustion track when you enter a dangerous situation alone, without backup or assistance.

nature.name: Cynic
nature.description: Clear your exhaustion track when you openly and directly ask dangerous questions about an accepted 'truth'.

drives.name: Discovery
drives.description: Advance when you encounter a new wonder or ruin in the forests.

drives.name: Freedom
drives.description: Advance when you free a group of denizens from oppression.

drives.name: Revenge
drives.description: Name your foe. Advance when you cause significant harm to them or their interests.

drives.name: Protection
drives.description: Name your ward. Advance when you protect them from significant danger, or when time passes and your ward is safe.

connections.name: Watcher
connections.description: When you figure them out, you always hold 1, even on a miss. When you plead with them to go along with you, you can let them clear 2-exhaustion instead of 1.

connections.name: Protector
connections.description: When they are in reach, mark exhaustion to take a blow meant for them. If you do, take +1 ongoing to weapon moves for the rest of the scene.

moves.name: Silent Paws
moves.description: You are adept at slipping into and out of dangerous situations without anyone noticing. When you attempt a roguish feat to sneak or hide, you can mark 2-exhaustion to shift a miss to a 7-9.

moves.name: Slip Away
moves.description: When you take advantage of an opening to escape from a dangerous situation, roll with Finesse. On a hit, you get away. On a 10+, choose 1. On a 7-9, choose 2: You suffer injury or exhaustion (GM's choice) during your escape; You end up in another dangerous situation; You leave something important behind. On a miss, you escape, but mark injury or exhaustion (GM's choice) and leave ample evidence for foes to track you.

moves.name: Poisons and Antidotes
moves.description: You have expertise in the poisons and antidotes of the Woodland. When you brew a poison, mark depletion and say what effect you want it to have: sleep, weakness, inebriation, or death. Any poison requires ingestion or injection. When you study a poison or its effects to make an antidote, the GM will tell you what special ingredient you'll need. Get the ingredient and mark depletion to brew the antidote.

moves.name: Forager
moves.description: When you travel or pass into a forest, before making any travel move, you can clear your choice of: up to 3-depletion; up to 2-exhaustion; up to 2-injury.

moves.name: Threatening Visage
moves.description: When you persuade an NPC with open threats or naked steel, roll with Might instead of Charm.

moves.name: Dirty Fighter
moves.description: Take two of the following weapon skills: Trick Shot, Confuse Senses, Improvise Weapon, Disarm, Vicious Strike. None of the skills you take with this move count against your maximum for advancement.

skills.name: Cleave
skills.description: When you cleave armored foes at close range, mark exhaustion and roll with Might. On a hit, you smash through their defenses and equipment; inflict 3-wear. On a 7–9, you overextend your weapon or yourself: mark wear or end up in a bad spot, your choice.

skills.name: Confuse Senses
skills.description: When you throw something to confuse an opponent's senses at close or intimate range, roll with Finesse. On a hit, you've thrown them off balance, blinded them, deafened them, or confused them, and given yourself an opportunity. On a 10+, they have to take some time to get their bearings and restore their senses before they can act clearly again. On a 7–9, you have just a few moments.

skills.name: Disarm
skills.description: When you target an opponent's weapon with your strikes at close range, roll with Finesse. On a hit, they have to mark 2-exhaustion or lose their weapon—it's well out of reach. On a 10+, they have to mark 3-exhaustion instead of 2.

skills.name: Harry
skills.description: When you harry a group of enemies at far range, mark wear and roll with Cunning. On a 10+, both. On a 7–9, choose 1: inflict 2-morale harm; they are pinned or blocked.

skills.name: Improvise
skills.description: When you make a weapon out of improvised materials around you, roll with Cunning. On a hit, you make a weapon; the GM will tell you its range tag and at least one other beneficial tag based on the materials you used. On a 7–9, the weapon also has a weakness tag.

skills.name: Parry
skills.description: When you try to parry the attacks of an enemy at close range, mark exhaustion and roll with Finesse. On a hit, you consume their attention. On a 10+, all 3. On a 7–9, pick 1: you inflict morale or exhaustion harm (GM's choice); you disarm your opponent—their weapon is out of hand, but in reach; you don't suffer any harm.

skills.name: Quick Shot
skills.description: When you fire a snap shot at an enemy at close range, roll with Luck. On a hit, inflict injury. On a 7–9, choose 1. On a 10+, choose 2: you don't mark wear; you don't mark exhaustion; you move quickly and change your position (and, if you choose, range); you keep your target at bay—they don't move.

skills.name: Storm a Group
skills.description: When you storm a group of foes in melee, mark exhaustion and roll with Might. On a hit, trade harm. On a 10+, choose 2. On a 7–9, choose 1: you show them up—you inflict 2-morale harm; you keep them off-balance and confused—you inflict 2-exhaustion; you avoid their blows to the best of your ability—you suffer little (-1) harm; you use them against each other—mark exhaustion again and they inflict their harm against themselves.

skills.name: Trick Shot
skills.description: When you fire a clever shot designed to take advantage of the environment at any range, mark wear and roll with Finesse. On a 7–9, choose 2. On a 10+, choose 3: your shot lands in any target of your choice within range, even if it's behind cover or hidden; your shot strikes a second available target of your choice; your shot cuts something, breaks something, or knocks something over—your choice; your shot distracts an opponent and provides an opportunity.

skills.name: Vicious Strike
skills.description: When you viciously strike an opponent where they are weak at intimate or close range, mark exhaustion and roll with Might. On a hit, they suffer serious (+1) harm and cannot mark wear on their armor to block it. On a 10+, you get away with the strike. On a 7–9, they score a blow against you as well.

feats.name: Blindside
feats.description: Backstab, murder, sneak attack, sucker punch.

feats.name: Pick Lock
feats.description: Open a locked door, chest, etc.

feats.name: Sneak
feats.description: Get into or out of places without being seen.

description: You are a skilled, willful vagabond, formerly a servant of a lord in a different land, now masterless. You came to the Woodland to live as a free vagabond.

background.name: Where do you now call home?
Why are you a vagabond?
answers: I want to build a masterless life
I seek a cause to redeem myself
I aim to bring a hunted foe to justice
I am hunted by old foes
I need freedom to fulfill my master's last wish

background.name: What happened to your last master?
Which faction have you served the most?
With which faction have you earned a special enmity?

nature.name: Survivor
nature.description: Clear your exhaustion track when you try to flee or cover allies' flight from a dangerous or overwhelming situation.

nature.name: Pilgrim
nature.description: Clear your exhaustion track when you find an expert in a skill you don't possess.

drives.name: Principles
drives.description: Advance when you express or embody your moral principles at great cost to yourself or your allies.

drives.name: Revenge
drives.description: Name your foe. Advance when you cause significant harm to them or their interests.

drives.name: Thrills
drives.description: Advance when you escape from certain death or incarceration.

drives.name: Wanderlust
drives.description: Advance when you finish a journey to a clearing.

connections.name: Partner
connections.description: When you fill in this connection, you each mark 2-prestige with the faction you helped, and mark 2-notoriety with the faction you harmed. During play, if you are spotted together, then any prestige or notoriety gains with those factions are doubled for the two of you.

connections.name: Watcher
connections.description: When you figure them out, you always hold 1, even on a miss. When you plead with them to go along with you, you can let them clear 2-exhaustion instead of 1.

moves.name: Always Armed
moves.description: Take the weapon skill Improvise a Weapon (it does not count against your limit). When you deal harm with an improvised weapon, deal +1 harm.

moves.name: Knowing a Lord's Will
moves.description: When you figure out a denizen of status, authority, or power, roll with Might instead of Charm. When you trick a denizen of status, authority, or power by playing subordinate, roll with Might instead of Cunning.

moves.name: Well-Mannered
moves.description: When you enter a social environment dependent on manners and etiquette, roll with Cunning. On a 10+, hold 3. On a 7-9, hold 2. Lose all hold when you leave or when social rules fall apart. Spend hold 1-for-1 to: cover up a social faux pas (clear 1-exhaustion); call out someone else's faux pas (inflict 1-morale harm); charm someone (take +1 ongoing to speak to them); demonstrate your value (mark prestige with a powerful denizen's faction). On a miss, mark exhaustion as you commit a gravely impolite error.

moves.name: Fealty
moves.description: When you commit yourself to the cause of someone you deem worthy, swear an oath stating what task you will complete on their behalf. Mark exhaustion to reroll a move made in pursuit of that task. You cannot commit to another cause until you accomplish the first, or break your oath. If you break your oath, fill your exhaustion track and mark 4-notoriety. If you fulfill your oath, mark 4-prestige.

moves.name: The Rules of War
moves.description: When you call upon a reasonable foe to uphold a rule of war, roll with Might. On a hit, they feel obliged; choose one they must follow. On a 7-9, they choose one you must follow as well. Rules: show mercy to surrendering foes; refrain from underhanded tactics; face each other without aid; keep violence from the unarmed; fight to surrender without retreat. On a miss, they feel no obligation.

moves.name: Always Watching
moves.description: Take +1 Cunning (max +3).

skills.name: Cleave
skills.description: When you cleave armored foes at close range, mark exhaustion and roll with Might. On a hit, you smash through their defenses and equipment; inflict 3-wear. On a 7–9, you overextend your weapon or yourself: mark wear or end up in a bad spot, your choice.

skills.description: When you throw something to confuse an opponent's senses at close or intimate range, roll with Finesse. On a hit, you've thrown them off balance, blinded them, deafened them, or confused them, and given yourself an opportunity. On a 10+, they have to take some time to get their bearings and restore their senses before they can act clearly again. On a 7–9, you have just a few moments.

skills.name: Harry
skills.description: When you harry a group of enemies at far range, mark wear and roll with Cunning. On a 10+, both. On a 7–9, choose 1: inflict 2-morale harm; they are pinned or blocked.

skills.name: Improvise
skills.description: When you make a weapon out of improvised materials around you, roll with Cunning. On a hit, you make a weapon; the GM will tell you its range tag and at least one other beneficial tag based on the materials you used. On a 7–9, the weapon also has a weakness tag.

On a 7–9, pick 1: you inflict morale or exhaustion harm (GM's choice); you disarm your opponent—their weapon is out of hand, but in reach; you don't suffer any harm.

On a 10+, choose 2: you don't mark wear; you don't mark exhaustion; you move quickly and change your position (and, if you choose, range); you keep your target at bay—they don't move.

skills.name: Storm a Group

On a 7–9, choose 1: you show them up—you inflict 2-morale harm; you keep them off-balance and confused—you inflict 2-exhaustion; you avoid their blows to the best of your ability—you suffer little (-1) harm; you use them against each other—mark exhaustion again and they inflict their harm against themselves.

skills.name: Trick Shot
skills.description: When you fire a clever shot designed to take advantage of the environment at any range, mark wear and roll with Finesse. On a 7–9, choose 2. On a 10+, choose 3: your shot lands in any target of your choice within range, even if it's behind cover or hidden; your shot strikes a second available target of your choice; your shot cuts something, breaks something, or knocks something over—your choice; your shot distracts an opponent and provides an opportunity.

skills.description: When you viciously strike an opponent where they are weak at intimate or close range, mark exhaustion and roll with Might. On a hit, they suffer serious (+1) harm and cannot mark wear on their armor to block it. On a 10+, you get away with the strike. On a 7–9, they score a blow against you as well.

feats.name: Blindside
feats.description: Backstab, murder, sneak attack, sucker punch.

feats.name: Pick Lock
feats.description: Open a locked door, chest, etc.

feats.name: Sneak
feats.description: Get into or out of places without being seen.

description: You are a lucky, dangerous vagabond, acting more as destroyer and troublemaker than anything else, perhaps creating chaos and destruction for its own sake.

background.name: Why are you a vagabond?
answers: I am on the run for a destructive crime
I seek vengeance for my suffering
I wish to defeat a faction
I am mistrusted by other denizens
I want to be free from society's bonds

background.name: Which faction have you served the most?
With which faction have you earned a special enmity?

nature.name: Arsonist
nature.description: Clear your exhaustion track when you use needlessly destructive or damaging methods to solve a problem.

nature.name: Combative
nature.description: Clear your exhaustion track when you try to start a fight against overwhelming opposition.

drives.name: Chaos
drives.description: Advance when you topple a tyrannical or dangerously overbearing figure or order.

drives.name: Thrills
drives.description: Advance when you escape from certain death or incarceration.

drives.name: Crime
drives.description: Advance when you illicitly score a significant prize or pull off an illegal caper against impressive odds.

drives.name: Infamy
drives.description: Advance when you decrease your reputation with any faction.

connections.name: Friend
connections.description: When you help them, you can mark 2-exhaustion to give a +2, instead of 1-exhaustion for a +1.

connections.name: Partner
connections.description: When you fill in this connection, you each mark 2-prestige with the faction you helped, and mark 2-notoriety with the faction you harmed. During play, if you are spotted together, then any prestige or notoriety gains with those factions are doubled for the two of you.

moves.name: Arsonist
moves.description: When you wreck something with flagrantly dangerous means (explosives, uncontrolled flame, etc.), roll with Luck instead of Might.

moves.name: Create to Destroy
moves.description: When you use available materials to rig up a dangerous device, roll with Finesse. On a hit, you cobble together something that will do what you want, one time. On a 10+, choose one. On a 7-9, choose two. The device is: more dangerous than intended; larger or more unwieldy than intended; more temperamental and fragile than intended. On a miss, you need some vital component to finish it.

moves.name: It's a Distraction!
moves.description: You gain the roguish feat Blindside (it does not count against your limit). When you attempt a roguish feat to blindside someone while they are distracted by environmental dangers, roll with Luck instead of Cunning.

moves.name: Daredevil
moves.description: You're at your luckiest when you go into danger without hesitation. When you dive into a dangerous situation without forethought or planning, treat yourself as having "Luck Armor" with 1 box of wear. The "Luck Armor" automatically goes away once the danger has passed.

moves.name: Danger Mask
moves.description: You have a mask or outfit worn when you go about your most destructive work. Treat it as equipment with two boxes of wear. While wearing your mask, any notoriety you gain is doubled, any prestige you gain is halved, and take +1 to trust fate and all Scoundrel playbook moves. If your mask is taken, mark exhaustion. If destroyed, mark 4-exhaustion.

moves.name: Better Lucky than Good
moves.description: When you use a weapon move (basic or skilled), mark exhaustion to roll with Luck instead of the listed stat.

skills.name: Cleave
skills.description: When you cleave armored foes at close range, mark exhaustion and roll with Might. On a hit, you smash through their defenses and equipment; inflict 3-wear. On a 7–9, you overextend your weapon or yourself: mark wear or end up in a bad spot, your choice.

skills.name: Confuse Senses
skills.description: When you throw something to confuse an opponent's senses at close or intimate range, roll with Finesse. On a hit, you've thrown them off balance, blinded them, deafened them, or confused them, and given yourself an opportunity. On a 10+, they have to take some time to get their bearings and restore their senses before they can act clearly again. On a 7–9, you have just a few moments.

skills.name: Disarm
skills.description: When you target an opponent's weapon with your strikes at close range, roll with Finesse. On a hit, they have to mark 2-exhaustion or lose their weapon—it's well out of reach. On a 10+, they have to mark 3-exhaustion instead of 2.

skills.name: Harry
skills.description: When you harry a group of enemies at far range, mark wear and roll with Cunning. On a 10+, both. On a 7–9, choose 1: inflict 2-morale harm; they are pinned or blocked.

skills.name: Improvise
skills.description: When you make a weapon out of improvised materials around you, roll with Cunning. On a hit, you make a weapon; the GM will tell you its range tag and at least one other beneficial tag based on the materials you used. On a 7–9, the weapon also has a weakness tag.

skills.name: Parry
skills.description: When you try to parry the attacks of an enemy at close range, mark exhaustion and roll with Finesse. On a hit, you consume their attention. On a 10+, all 3. On a 7–9, pick 1: you inflict morale or exhaustion harm (GM's choice); you disarm your opponent—their weapon is out of hand, but in reach; you don't suffer any harm.

skills.name: Quick Shot
skills.description: When you fire a snap shot at an enemy at close range, roll with Luck. On a hit, inflict injury. On a 7–9, choose 1. On a 10+, choose 2: you don't mark wear; you don't mark exhaustion; you move quickly and change your position (and, if you choose, range); you keep your target at bay—they don't move.

skills.name: Storm a Group
skills.description: When you storm a group of foes in melee, mark exhaustion and roll with Might. On a hit, trade harm. On a 10+, choose 2. On a 7–9, choose 1: you show them up—you inflict 2-morale harm; you keep them off-balance and confused—you inflict 2-exhaustion; you avoid their blows to the best of your ability—you suffer little (-1) harm; you use them against each other—mark exhaustion again and they inflict their harm against themselves.

skills.name: Trick Shot
skills.description: When you fire a clever shot designed to take advantage of the environment at any range, mark wear and roll with Finesse. On a 7–9, choose 2. On a 10+, choose 3: your shot lands in any target of your choice within range, even if it's behind cover or hidden; your shot strikes a second available target of your choice; your shot cuts something, breaks something, or knocks something over—your choice; your shot distracts an opponent and provides an opportunity.

skills.name: Vicious Strike
skills.description: When you viciously strike an opponent where they are weak at intimate or close range, mark exhaustion and roll with Might. On a hit, they suffer serious (+1) harm and cannot mark wear on their armor to block it. On a 10+, you get away with the strike. On a 7–9, they score a blow against you as well.

feats.name: Blindside
feats.description: Backstab, murder, sneak attack, sucker punch.

feats.name: Pick Lock
feats.description: Open a locked door, chest, etc.

feats.name: Sneak
feats.description: Get into or out of places without being seen.

description: You are a cunning, criminal vagabond, capable of stealing even the most well-guarded treasures, perhaps committed to crime and theft for its own sake.

background.name: Why are you a vagabond?
answers: I have no better way to get food, water, shelter, and money
I am on the run from associates
I am mistrusted by other denizens
I am being hunted by a powerful official

background.name: Which faction have you served the most?
With which faction have you earned a special enmity?

nature.name: Kleptomaniac
nature.description: Clear your exhaustion track when you try to selfishly steal something valuable or important.

nature.name: Rebellious
nature.description: Clear your exhaustion track when you grievously insult, defy, or anger figures of authority.

drives.name: Freedom
drives.description: Advance when you free a group of denizens from oppression.

drives.name: Greed
drives.description: Advance when you secure a serious payday or treasure.

drives.name: Ambition
drives.description: Advance when you increase your reputation with any faction.

drives.name: Thrills
drives.description: Advance when you escape from certain death or incarceration.

connections.name: Professional
connections.description: If you share information with them after reading a tense situation, you both benefit from the +1 for acting on the answers. If you help them while they attempt a roguish feat, you gain choices on the help move as if you had marked 2-exhaustion when you mark 1-exhaustion.

connections.name: Friend
connections.description: When you help them, you can mark 2-exhaustion to give a +2, instead of 1-exhaustion for a +1.

moves.name: Breaking and Entering
moves.description: When you attempt roguish feats to get into or out of a place you've previously been, you can mark exhaustion to make the move as if you had rolled a 10+, instead of rolling.

moves.name: Disappear Into the Dark
moves.description: When you slip into shadows while unnoticed, mark exhaustion and hold 1. As long as you remain quiet, move slowly, and hold 1 for this move, you will remain hidden. If you inadvertently reveal yourself, lose your hold. Spend your hold to reveal yourself from a darkened place suddenly and without warning. If you attack someone immediately after spending the hold, take +3 on the roll.

moves.name: Rope-a-Dope
moves.description: When you evade and dodge your enemy so as to tire them out, roll with Finesse. On a hit, you can mark exhaustion to make them mark 2-exhaustion. On a 10+, you can mark exhaustion to make them mark 3-exhaustion. On a miss, they catch you in the middle of a dodge—you're at their mercy.

moves.name: Small Hands
moves.description: When you grapple with an enemy larger than you, roll with Finesse instead of Might. On a miss, they overpower you—you're at their mercy.

moves.name: Master Thief
moves.description: Take +1 Finesse (max +3).

moves.name: Nose for Gold
moves.description: When you figure someone out, you can always ask (even on a miss): what is the most valuable thing they are carrying? When you read a tense situation, you can always ask (even on a miss): what is the most valuable thing here?

skills.name: Cleave
skills.description: When you cleave armored foes at close range, mark exhaustion and roll with Might. On a hit, you smash through their defenses and equipment; inflict 3-wear. On a 7–9, you overextend your weapon or yourself: mark wear or end up in a bad spot, your choice.

skills.name: Confuse Senses
skills.description: When you throw something to confuse an opponent's senses at close or intimate range, roll with Finesse. On a hit, you've thrown them off balance, blinded them, deafened them, or confused them, and given yourself an opportunity. On a 10+, they have to take some time to get their bearings and restore their senses before they can act clearly again. On a 7–9, you have just a few moments.

skills.description: When you harry a group of enemies at far range, mark wear and roll with Cunning. On a 10+, both. On a 7–9, choose 1: inflict 2-morale harm; they are pinned or blocked.

skills.name: Improvise
skills.description: When you make a weapon out of improvised materials around you, roll with Cunning. On a hit, you make a weapon; the GM will tell you its range tag and at least one other beneficial tag based on the materials you used. On a 7–9, the weapon also has a weakness tag.

On a 7–9, pick 1: you inflict morale or exhaustion harm (GM's choice); you disarm your opponent—their weapon is out of hand, but in reach; you don't suffer any harm.

On a 7–9, choose 1: you show them up—you inflict 2-morale harm; you keep them off-balance and confused—you inflict 2-exhaustion; you avoid their blows to the best of your ability—you suffer little (-1) harm; you use them against each other—mark exhaustion again and they inflict their harm against themselves.

On a 10+, choose 3: your shot lands in any target of your choice within range, even if it's behind cover or hidden; your shot strikes a second available target of your choice; your shot cuts something, breaks something, or knocks something over—your choice; your shot distracts an opponent and provides an opportunity.

skills.name: Vicious Strike
skills.description: When you viciously strike an opponent where they are weak at intimate or close range, mark exhaustion and roll with Might. On a hit, they suffer serious (+1) harm and cannot mark wear on their armor to block it. On a 10+, you get away with the strike. On a 7–9, they score a blow against you as well.

feats.name: Blindside
feats.description: Backstab, murder, sneak attack, sucker punch.

feats.name: Pick Lock
feats.description: Open a locked door, chest, etc.

feats.name: Sneak
feats.description: Get into or out of places without being seen.

description: You are an adept, clever vagabond, interested in mechanisms and craftsmanship, perhaps possessed of ideas that separate you from those around you.

background.name: Why are you a vagabond?
answers: I refuse to keep my ideas to myself
I need to rebuild my workshop anew in a safe place
I need to find and save my family
I need to keep my most dangerous design safe

background.name: Which faction have you served the most?
With which faction have you earned a special enmity?

nature.name: Perfectionist
nature.description: Clear your exhaustion track when you replace someone else's existing tool or resource with something truly great.

nature.name: Radical
nature.description: Clear your exhaustion track when you espouse dangerous ideas to the wrong audience.

drives.name: Greed
drives.description: Advance when you secure a serious payday or treasure.

drives.name: Ambition
drives.description: Advance when you increase your reputation with any faction.

drives.name: Revenge
drives.description: Name your foe. Advance when you cause significant harm to them or their interests.

drives.name: Protection
drives.description: Name your ward. Advance when you protect them from significant danger, or when time passes and your ward is safe.

connections.name: Professional
connections.description: If you share information with them after reading a tense situation, you both benefit from the +1 for acting on the answers. If you help them while they attempt a roguish feat, you gain choices on the help move as if you had marked 2-exhaustion when you mark 1-exhaustion.

connections.name: Family
connections.description: When you help them fulfill their nature, you both clear your exhaustion track.

moves.name: Toolbox
moves.description: You have a kit of tools and supplies. Choose two features: assorted scrap wood, assorted gears and springs, esoteric hand tools, manuals, assorted medicines, portable alchemy kit, sewing kit, cookware, minor explosives. Choose one drawback: heavy, bulky & obvious, stolen, fragile. When you open up your toolkit and dedicate yourself to making a thing or getting to the bottom of something, the GM will give you 1 to 4 conditions to accomplish your goal.

moves.name: Repair
moves.description: When you repair destroyed personal equipment with your toolbox, the GM will set one condition. Fulfill it and clear all wear. When you repair damaged personal equipment, spend depletion or Value 1-for-1 for each box of wear you clear.

moves.name: Big Pockets
moves.description: Take two extra boxes of depletion.

moves.name: Jury Rig
moves.description: When you create a makeshift device on the fly, roll with Cunning. On a hit, you create a device that works once, then breaks. On a 10+, choose one: it works exceptionally well; you get an additional use out of it. On a miss, the device works but has an unintended side effect.

moves.name: Nimble Mind
moves.description: When you attempt roguish feats involving mechanisms or locks, mark depletion to roll with Cunning instead of Finesse.

moves.name: Dismantle
moves.description: When you dismantle a broken or disabled piece of equipment or machinery, clear 2-depletion.

skills.name: Cleave
skills.description: When you cleave armored foes at close range, mark exhaustion and roll with Might. On a hit, you smash through their defenses and equipment; inflict 3-wear. On a 7–9, you overextend your weapon or yourself: mark wear or end up in a bad spot, your choice.

On a 10+, they have to take some time to get their bearings and restore their senses before they can act clearly again. On a 7–9, you have just a few moments.

skills.name: Disarm
skills.description: When you target an opponent's weapon with your strikes at close range, roll with Finesse. On a hit, they have to mark 2-exhaustion or lose their weapon—it's well out of reach. On a 10+, they have to mark 3-exhaustion instead of 2.

skills.description: When you harry a group of enemies at far range, mark wear and roll with Cunning. On a 10+, both. On a 7–9, choose 1: inflict 2-morale harm; they are pinned or blocked.

skills.name: Improvise
skills.description: When you make a weapon out of improvised materials around you, roll with Cunning. On a hit, you make a weapon; the GM will tell you its range tag and at least one other beneficial tag based on the materials you used. On a 7–9, the weapon also has a weakness tag.

skills.description: When you try to parry the attacks of an enemy at close range, mark exhaustion and roll with Finesse. On a hit, you consume their attention. On a 10+, all 3. On a 7–9, pick 1: you inflict morale or exhaustion harm (GM's choice); you disarm your opponent—their weapon is out of hand, but in reach; you don't suffer any harm.

skills.description: When you fire a snap shot at an enemy at close range, roll with Luck. On a hit, inflict injury. On a 7–9, choose 1. On a 10+, choose 2: you don't mark wear; you don't mark exhaustion; you move quickly and change your position (and, if you choose, range); you keep your target at bay—they don't move.

On a 7–9, choose 1: you show them up—you inflict 2-morale harm; you keep them off-balance and confused—you inflict 2-exhaustion; you avoid their blows to the best of your ability—you suffer little (-1) harm; you use them against each other—mark exhaustion again and they inflict their harm against themselves.

skills.name: Trick Shot
skills.description: When you fire a clever shot designed to take advantage of the environment at any range, mark wear and roll with Finesse. On a 7–9, choose 2. On a 10+, choose 3: your shot lands in any target of your choice within range, even if it's behind cover or hidden; your shot strikes a second available target of your choice; your shot cuts something, breaks something, or knocks something over—your choice; your shot distracts an opponent and provides an opportunity.

skills.name: Vicious Strike
skills.description: When you viciously strike an opponent where they are weak at intimate or close range, mark exhaustion and roll with Might. On a hit, they suffer serious (+1) harm and cannot mark wear on their armor to block it. On a 10+, you get away with the strike. On a 7–9, they score a blow against you as well.

feats.name: Blindside
feats.description: Backstab, murder, sneak attack, sucker punch.

feats.name: Pick Lock
feats.description: Open a locked door, chest, etc.

feats.name: Sneak
feats.description: Get into or out of places without being seen.

description: You are a charming, survivor vagabond, using words to get out of dangerous situations, perhaps even setting possible predators upon each other to keep them away from yourself.

background.name: Why are you a vagabond?
answers: I am being hunted by a powerful vagabond
I can't settle down with the denizen I truly love
I seek to depose corrupt and dangerous leaders
I am on the run for my lies

background.name: Which faction have you served the most?
With which faction have you earned a special enmity?

nature.name: Glutton
nature.description: Clear your exhaustion track when you overindulge on vices like drink, food, and gambling.

nature.name: Hustler
nature.description: Clear your exhaustion track when you try to spring a con on a powerful or dangerous mark.

drives.name: Chaos
drives.description: Advance when you topple a tyrannical or dangerously overbearing figure or order.

drives.name: Thrills
drives.description: Advance when you escape from certain death or incarceration.

drives.name: Clean Paws
drives.description: Advance when you accomplish an illicit, criminal goal while maintaining a believable veneer of innocence.

drives.name: Wanderlust
drives.description: Advance when you finish a journey to a clearing.

connections.name: Family
connections.description: When you help them fulfill their nature, you both clear your exhaustion track.

connections.name: Watcher
connections.description: When you figure them out, you always hold 1, even on a miss. When you plead with them to go along with you, you can let them clear 2-exhaustion instead of 1.

moves.name: Instigator
moves.description: When you trick an NPC into fighting another NPC, you can remove one option from the 7-9 list—they cannot choose that option instead of doing what you want.

moves.name: Pleasant Facade
moves.description: When you suck up to or otherwise butter up an unsuspecting NPC, roll with Charm. On a 10+, hold 3. On a 7-9, hold 2. Spend your hold 1-for-1 to deflect their suspicion or aggression away from you onto someone or something else. On a miss, your attempts at flattery are suspicious—they're going to keep their eye on you.

moves.name: Desperate Smile
moves.description: When you trust fate to see you through by begging, pleading, or abasing yourself, roll with Charm instead of Luck.

moves.name: Charm Offensive
moves.description: When you play upon an enemy's insecurities, concerns, or fears to distract them with words during a fight, roll with Cunning. On a hit, you create an opening—make any available weapon move against them at +1, or strike quickly and deal injury. On a 7-9, you also tick them off; they aren't listening to you anymore until the situation drastically changes. On a miss, you infuriate them—they come at you hard.

moves.name: Let's Play
moves.description: When you play a game of skill and wit to loosen another's tongue, roll with Charm. On a hit, they let slip something useful or valuable. On a 7-9, you have to lose the game to get them there; mark depletion. On a miss, either mark depletion and cut your losses, or mark 3-depletion and they'll start talking.

moves.name: Pocket Sand
moves.description: Take the weapon skill Confuse Senses (it does not count against your limit). When you throw something to confuse an opponent's senses at close or intimate range, roll with Cunning instead of Finesse.

skills.name: Cleave
skills.description: When you cleave armored foes at close range, mark exhaustion and roll with Might. On a hit, you smash through their defenses and equipment; inflict 3-wear. On a 7–9, you overextend your weapon or yourself: mark wear or end up in a bad spot, your choice.

On a hit, you've thrown them off balance, blinded them, deafened them, or confused them, and given yourself an opportunity. On a 10+, they have to take some time to get their bearings and restore their senses before they can act clearly again. On a 7–9, you have just a few moments.

skills.name: Harry
skills.description: When you harry a group of enemies at far range, mark wear and roll with Cunning. On a 10+, both. On a 7–9, choose 1: inflict 2-morale harm; they are pinned or blocked.

skills.name: Improvise

feats.name: Blindside
feats.description: Backstab, murder, sneak attack, sucker punch.

feats.name: Pick Lock
feats.description: Open a locked door, chest, etc.

feats.name: Sneak
feats.description: Get into or out of places without being seen.
